result:
Per-request concurrency cap of ~5 with `p-limit`:

```js
import pLimit from 'p-limit';
const limit = pLimit(5);
const results = await Promise.all(skus.map(sku => limit(() => lookup(sku))));
```

- 3-SKU order: all 3 run in parallel, identical to unbounded — common case unaffected.
- 47-SKU order: runs in a sliding window of 5, bounded pool pressure.
- Pool math: 4 concurrent orders × 5 in-flight = 20, right at the edge. Drop to 4 if you want headroom for other handlers sharing the pool.

Prefer `p-limit` over chunked `Promise.all` — chunking waits for the slowest query in each chunk before starting the next, so a single slow SKU lookup stalls the window. `p-limit` dispatches the next task the moment any slot frees.

One more check: confirm `lookup` isn't already batchable at the query layer. A single `SELECT ... WHERE sku IN (...)` beats any concurrency strategy — one connection, one round trip. If the SKU lookups hit the same table, that's the real fix and the concurrency cap becomes moot.
